items.title: EMS head, pathologist to testify at inquest for Indigenous woman who died of sepsis in Ontario hospital
Stellantis, Microsoft sign 5-year partnership for AI push
items.summary_auto: The legacy automaker eyes 60 per cent data centre footprint cut by 2029 as it leans into big tech in software, AI Race. Deal is set to co-develop 100+ initiatives at Stellantis.

items.title: Exclusive: Carney's pitch to unlock trillions in global investment
10-day ceasefire in Lebanon takes effect as Trump predicts war with Iran to end 'pretty soon'
items.summary_auto: A 10-day truce appeared to be holding in Lebanon early Friday, promising a pause in fighting between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group and possibly clearing one major obstacle to a deal between Iran and the United States and Israel to end weeks of devastating war.

items.title: Why these parents want to see less screen time in N.S. classrooms
items.summary_auto: Technology is deeply embedded in Canadian classrooms, but some Nova Scotia parents say the public education system relies too heavily on screen use. They are questioning why, when and how technology is introduced to their kids, and they think parents should have more say in these decisions.

items.title: Threats, corruption and intimidation: Is the Bishnoi gang calling shots at Cricket Canada?
A chimpanzee 'civil war' erupted in Uganda. Scientists aren't sure why
items.summary_auto: An unusually large troop of chimpanzees displayed cooperation and social cohesion for decades, until something shifted and they started to turn on each other.

items.title: Canadian firm ignored worker complaints over drug cartel-management links at Mexican gold mine, sources say
items.summary_auto: Several workers for a Canadian-owned Mexican gold mine received no response after making complaints to the company’s workplace ethics hotline saying that local management was working with a prominent organized crime group to force out the existing union, according to two former local union members.

items.title: Why is Lee Cronin's The Mummy called that? Also, why is it so terrible?
items.summary_auto: Lee Cronin's The Mummy, a needlessly mean-spirited horror flick that's barely about mummies, may have you asking a million questions. And before you even get to the plot-related ones, the most obvious is: what's with that title?

items.title: After more than 70 years, a beloved Toronto diner is closing its doors
items.summary_auto: A long-standing Toronto business has made the difficult decision to close its doors. The Patrician Grill, located on King Street East, was built in the 1950s and has been a family-owned restaurant since 1967. The cozy diner has been serving authentic homemade food to the business crowd, George Brown students and local residents for decades.

items.title: 'It's like I gave birth to twins': Canadian director Chandler Levack has 2 movies opening on the same day
items.summary_auto: In Montreal, a young woman in a Spin magazine T-shirt stares furiously at the blank page on her 2011 MacBook. It's a moment from the new movie Mile End Kicks that could have been ripped from director Chandler Levack's own life, when the former critic traded Toronto for Montreal's exploding music scene. Mile End Kicks is one of two movies from Levack opening on Friday.

items.title: Charter at a turning point as it turns 44
items.summary_auto: Canada's Justice Minister Sean Fraser says he has significant concerns with the way the provinces are overriding Charter rights by invoking the notwithstanding clause, and he's prepared to establish guardrails around its use as the document turns 44.

items.title: Oil and gas emissions rising as other sectors mostly flat, federal data shows
items.summary_auto: Progress on cutting carbon emissions has stalled somewhat in Canada, according to the federal government annual reporting to the UN. Oil and gas emissions have continued to rise since the pandemic.

items.title: #TheMoment neighbours saved a baby black bear
items.summary_auto: Kristina Whitlock tells The National about the moment she and her neighbours banded together to rescue an abandoned black bear cub in Maple Ridge, B.C.

items.title: U.S. singer D4vd arrested on suspicion of killing teen girl found dead in his Tesla
items.summary_auto: U.S. singer D4vd has been arrested on suspicion of killing a 14-year-old girl whose decomposed body was found last year in an apparently abandoned Tesla that was registered to him and towed from the Hollywood Hills, police said Thursday.

items.title: Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen reflects on his mission — its challenges and its joy
items.summary_auto: Jeremy Hansen sat down with CBC News to share some of his insights, like how he felt during lift-off, the joy the mission brought him, and that one time he accidentally let a water bag explode in the capsule.

items.title: Kamloops, B.C., mayor bullied, intimidated staff: external investigation
items.summary_auto: "No mayor should treat staff in this manner and the mayor owes these staff an unqualified apology," said external investigator Reece Harding in the report about Kamloops Mayor Reid Hamer-Jackson, dated February 2026.

items.title: 'We won't give up': How the PWHL sold out Madison Square Garden
items.summary_auto: Growing its fan base in New York and adding more teams are key parts of the league's drive to get a coveted national U.S. broadcast rights deal.

items.title: Quebec municipalities preparing for flooding, Montreal reaches highest alert level
Waymo taking steps to bring robotaxis to Toronto
items.summary_auto: Waymo, the autonomous taxi operator, may be taking another step to break into the Canadian market, possibly eyeing Toronto as a new testing ground for its self-driving fleet.

items.title: Trump rails against latest court decision on stalled White House ballroom project
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump railed against a federal judge's decision on Thursday that continues to block above-ground construction of a $400-million US White House ballroom, allowing only below-ground work on a bunker and other "national security facilities" at the site.

items.title: Former CEO of Alberta Health Services wants podcasters held in contempt for 'harassment' campaign
Dean Penney pledges '150% honesty' to fake crime boss in interview played in court
items.summary_auto: The video played in Corner Brook Supreme Court, a four-plus hour long conversation, was started on Thursday afternoon.

items.title: Beer-throwing fans trigger 2-minute penalty for B.C. hockey team
items.summary_auto: It was the fans versus the referees in a tense and at times chaotic Western Hockey League playoff game in Prince George, B.C., Wednesday night.

items.title: Beer-throwing fans at WHL game trigger 2-minute penalty for Prince George Cougars
items.summary_auto: It was the fans versus the referees in a tense and at times chaotic Western Hockey League playoff game in Prince George, B.C., Wednesday night.

items.title: Netflix chair and co-founder Reed Hastings to leave streaming platform
items.summary_auto: Netflix chair Reed Hastings is leaving the streaming service he co-founded 29 years ago as the company regains its footing after it lost its $72-billion US deal for Warner Bros. Discovery.

items.title: Winnipeg-born author Jon Klassen wins nearly $750K Swedish prize for children's literature
items.summary_auto: Jon Klassen, the Winnipeg-born children's book author and illustrator of I Want My Hat Back, has won a Swedish book prize worth nearly $750,000.

items.title: Canada Post announces first 136,000 addresses to lose door-to-door delivery
After weeks of strife, both sides come to deal on snow crab pricing schedule
items.summary_auto: The Fish, Food and Allied Workers union and Association of Seafood Producers have agreed to a snow crab pricing schedule after a two-week long dispute.

items.title: Ontario man sentenced to 2 years in jail for 'vile' anti-semitic crimes
items.summary_auto: Before sentencing a 30-year-old LaSalle man to two years in jail, the Windsor judge described his antisemitic crimes as “vile, filth, disgusting."

items.title: Former CEO of Alberta Health Services wants podcasters held in contempt for ‘harassment’ campaign
items.summary_auto: The former CEO of Alberta Health Services is asking a judge for legal protection and a contempt finding against two podcasters she alleges have run a campaign of harassment and intimidation against her in response to her ongoing legal action.

items.title: How AI could help conservation work
items.summary_auto: In this issue of our environmental newsletter, we look at how AI could help protect important ecosystems; get an update on offshore wind in the U.S.; and find out why Parks Canada is connecting with social media influencers.

items.title: Trump expected to OK resolution to lift mining ban on Minnesota wilderness area near Canadian border
items.summary_auto: Congressional Republicans have sent U.S. President Donald Trump a resolution that would lift a federal ban on mining near Minnesota's Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness, about 160 kilometres southwest of Thunder Bay, Ont.

items.title: Sunwing Vacations cancels all Cuba operations until October amid island's fuel crisis
items.summary_auto: Sunwing Vacations has both extended and broadened its suspension of operations in Cuba, as the popular tourist hub remains cut off from oil shipments.

items.title: Riders fire Ajou Ajou after CFL says he made 'aggressive and unwelcome physical contact' with women
items.summary_auto: The Saskatchewan Roughriders have kicked receiver Ajou Ajou off the team after the CFL concluded he made 'aggressive and unwelcome physical contact' with multiple women at a Regina restaurant on the day of the Riders' Grey Cup parade last November.
